default_annotator.report_text:
Anunak

Primary remote access toolkit (RAT) for monitoring victim systems of interest Use of remote access software to control victims' machines.

It contains all the functionality you would expect from a RAT, allowing the adversary to execute commands, manage the file system, manage processes, and collect data Using remote access software to execute commands and manage victim systems.

it can record videos of victim sessions Capture of compromised system screenshots

log keystrokes Capture of keyboard inputs.

enable remote desktop Using RDP to access and control the victim's system.

install Ammyy Admin or VNC modules Use of external remote services to maintain compromised system access.

Agent ORM

The malware collects basic system information Collection of basic system information.

take screenshots of victim systems Capture of compromised system screenshots

It is used to download next-stage payloads when systems of interest are identified Transfer of additional tools or payloads to the compromised system.

VB Flash

Several versions of VB Flash were developed including ones that utilized Google Forms, Google Macros, and Google Spreadsheets together to make a command-and-control (C2) channel Use of legitimate web services for command and control.

This variant would POST victim data to a specified Google form, then make a request to a Google macro script, receiving an address for a Google Spreadsheet from which to request commands Data exfiltration through legitimate web services.

JS Flash

JS Flash capabilities closely resemble those of VB Flash and leverage interesting techniques in deployment via batch scripts embedded as OLE objects in malicious documents Using the Windows command shell to execute commands and scripts.

minor changes to obfuscation Obfuscation of files or information to avoid detection.

ability to downloadTinyMet(a cutdown of theMetasploit Meterpreterpayload) Transfer of additional tools or payloads to the compromised system.

PowerShell was also used heavily for the execution of commands and arbitrary script execution Using PowerShell to execute arbitrary commands and scripts.

Bateleur

download arbitrary scripts and executables, deploy TinyMet Transfer of additional tools or payloads to the compromised system.

execute commands via PowerShell Using PowerShell to execute commands.

deploy a credential stealer Extraction of credentials from password repositories

collect victim system information Collection of system information.

spear phishing emails to deploy malicious documents Use of spear phishing attachments to gain initial access.

used exploits Using exploits to execute code on the victim's machine.

they have used macros and OLE embedded objects Use of malicious documents that require user intervention for execution.

spear phishing emails Use of spear phishing attachments to gain initial access.

that use exploits for a variety of vulnerabilities in Microsoft Office: CVE-2015-2545 CVE-2014-4114 CVE-2015-1770 CVE-2015-1641 Using exploits to execute code on the victim's machine.
